summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on product + fintech innovation.

articles.title: Engine aims to fix SMB cards
articles.description: Engine, a business-travel startup, says its smaller-business customers aren’t served by current travel and entertainment card players in the market.

articles.title: Global Payments feels Middle East impact
articles.description: The payments services provider said the conflict in the Middle East is taking a toll as some of its airline customers are impacted.

articles.title: Cash-strapped homeowners tap BNPL
articles.description: Researchers at the JPMorgan Chase Institute found that homeowners are most likely to use buy now, pay later tools when their bank accounts are low or their credit cards are maxed out.

articles.title: PayPal pivots for 2026
articles.description: The digital payment player aims to keep scaling its collection of integrated businesses, the company’s chief financial and operating officer said.

articles.title: Shift4 promotes Global Blue execs
articles.description: The payments processor promoted three executives to leadership roles, naming Pier Francesco Nervini as head of its international division.

articles.title: Meta revamps ad payment policy
articles.description: The social media company is mandating that some of its advertisers make their monthly payments via invoice or debit starting next month.

articles.title: Par Tech faces investor pressure
articles.description: One of the point-of-sale software company’s largest shareholders began urging it to consider “strategic alternatives,” such as a sale, last week.

articles.title: AWS leaps into agentic payments
articles.description: Amazon Web Services, the tech giant’s cloud computing network, is gearing up for armies of AI-powered agents and transactions.
